---
title: "Using LuaLaTeX"
---

## What is LuaLaTeX?

**LuaLaTeX** is a modern TeX engine that extends pdfLaTeX with the [Lua](https://www.lua.org/) scripting language. It is the recommended engine for most contemporary LaTeX projects because it supports:

- **Unicode natively** — no need for `inputenc` or `fontenc`
- **OpenType and TrueType fonts** via the `fontspec` package
- **Lua scripting** for dynamic document generation
- **Full backwards compatibility** with most pdfLaTeX documents
- Better support for complex scripts (Arabic, CJK, etc.)

LuaLaTeX is bundled with all major TeX distributions (TeX Live, MiKTeX) so chances are that you already have LuaLaTeX on your computer. 

::: {.callout-important}
For accessible tagging to work, you need to use a **current version** of LuaLaTeX. Please refer to the documentation of the TeX-distribution or IDE you are using for instructions on how to update LuaLaTeX to the latest release.
:::
---

## Set LuaLaTeX as Your Engine in Popular IDEs

### TeXShop (macOS)

TeXShop is the classic macOS LaTeX editor included with MacTeX.

**Per-document (recommended):**

Add this magic comment at the top of your `.tex` file:

```latex
% !TEX program = lualatex
```

**As the default engine:**

1. Open **TeXShop → Preferences**
2. Go to the **Engine** tab
3. Under *Default Command*, select **LuaLaTeX** from the dropdown
4. Click **OK**

### TeXworks (Windows, macOS, Linux)

TeXworks is a lightweight cross-platform editor bundled with both TeX Live and MiKTeX.

**Per-document:**

```latex
% !TEX program = lualatex
```

**As the default engine:**

1. Go to **Edit → Preferences → Typesetting**
2. Under *Default program*, select **LuaLaTeX**
3. Click **OK**

### TeXstudio (Windows, macOS, Linux)

TeXstudio is one of the most popular full-featured LaTeX editors.

**Per-document:**

```latex
% !TeX program = lualatex
```

**As the default compiler:**

1. Open **Options → Configure TeXstudio**
2. Go to **Build**
3. Under *Default Compiler*, select **LuaLaTeX** from the dropdown
4. Click **OK**

::: {.callout-tip}
TeXstudio also supports setting the bibliography tool and other build options in the same Build settings panel.
:::

### Overleaf (Web-based)

[Overleaf](https://www.overleaf.com) is the leading cloud-based LaTeX editor — no installation required.

**Per-project:**

1. Open your project
2. Click the **Menu** button (☰) in the top-left corner
3. Under **Settings → Compiler**, select **LuaLaTeX**
4. Click anywhere to close — all future compilations will use LuaLaTeX

Alternatively, add the magic comment to your main `.tex` file:

```latex
% !TEX program = lualatex
```

### VS Code with LaTeX Workshop (Windows, macOS, Linux)

[LaTeX Workshop](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=James-Yu.latex-workshop) is the most popular LaTeX extension for VS Code.

**Per-document:**

```latex
% !TEX program = lualatex
```

**As the default recipe (settings.json):**

Open **Settings → Open Settings (JSON)** (`Ctrl+Shift+P` → *"Open User Settings JSON"`) and add:

```{.json filename="settings.json"}
{
  "latex-workshop.latex.recipes": [
    {
      "name": "LuaLaTeX",
      "tools": ["lualatex"]
    }
  ],
  "latex-workshop.latex.tools": [
    {
      "name": "lualatex",
      "command": "lualatex",
      "args": [
        "--shell-escape",
        "--interaction=nonstopmode",
        "--synctex=1",
        "%DOC%"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Emacs with AUCTeX (Windows, macOS, Linux)

**Per-document (file-local variables):**

Add this at the bottom of your `.tex` file:

```latex
%%% Local Variables:
%%% mode: latex
%%% TeX-engine: luatex
%%% End:
```

**Globally in your `init.el`:**

```{.lisp filename="init.el"}
(setq-default TeX-engine 'luatex)
```

**During a session:**

Use `M-x TeX-engine-set` and select `luatex`.

### Vim / Neovim with VimTeX (Windows, macOS, Linux)

**Per-document (modeline at top of file):**

```latex
% vim: set ft=tex spell spelllang=en_us:
```

**Global setting in `init.vim` / `init.lua`:**

```{.vim filename="init.vim"}
let g:vimtex_compiler_latexmk_engines = {'_': '-lualatex'}
```

```{.lua filename="init.lua"}
vim.g.vimtex_compiler_latexmk_engines = { _ = '-lualatex' }
```

---

## An Example LuaLaTeX Document

Here is a minimal LuaLaTeX document that you can use for testing your installation:

```{.latex filename="hello-lualatex.tex"}
\documentclass{article}

% LuaLaTeX-specific packages
\usepackage{fontspec}       % OpenType font support
\usepackage{luacode}        % Lua scripting in LaTeX

% Set a system font (change to one available on your system)
\setmainfont{Latin Modern Roman}

\title{My First LuaLaTeX Document}
\author{Your Name}
\date{\today}

\begin{document}

\maketitle

\section{Hello, LuaLaTeX!}

This document is compiled with LuaLaTeX.
It supports Unicode natively: café, naïve, Ångström, 日本語.

\section{Lua Scripting}

The answer to life is
\begin{luacode}
tex.sprint(6 * 7)
\end{luacode}.

\end{document}
```

---

## Common Issues and Fixes

| Problem | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `lualatex: command not found` | TeX not on PATH | Add TeX bin dir to your `PATH` |
| Font not found | System font not installed | Install font or use a TeX font |
| `! Package fontspec Error` | Using pdfLaTeX accidentally | Ensure LuaLaTeX is the selected engine |
| Slow compilation | LuaLaTeX is inherently slower | Use `--draftmode` for intermediate builds |
| Unicode characters not rendering | Missing font coverage | Use a broader font like Noto or DejaVu |

---

## Additional Resources

- [LuaLaTeX documentation (luatex.org)](http://www.luatex.org/documentation.html)
- [fontspec package documentation (CTAN)](https://ctan.org/pkg/fontspec)
- [TeX Live Guide](https://tug.org/texlive/doc/texlive-en/texlive-en.html)
- [LaTeX Wikibook — LuaLaTeX chapter](https://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/LaTeX/LuaLaTeX)
- [Overleaf LuaLaTeX documentation](https://www.overleaf.com/learn/latex/LuaLaTeX)
